---
title: "svf circuit"
summary: "draw a filter circuit and do frequency analysis."
---

# Create circuit

This will create the data and parameters to plot datas using D3.

## Usage

First create some datas for example in a python block.

```{python}
#| label: fig-sfv
#| fig-cap: "State variable filter"
#| fig-align: center


from recad import Circuit, Schema, Symbol, LocalLabel, Wire, Junction, Simulation
import numpy as np

draw = Schema("StateVariableFilter", ["/usr/share/kicad/symbols"])
draw = (
  draw
  + LocalLabel("INPUT").at((25.4, 50.80)).rotate(180)
  + Symbol("R1","100k", "Device:R").rotate(90) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y")
  + Symbol("C1","220n", "Device:C").rotate(90) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y")
  + (u1_dot_in := Junction())
  + Symbol("U1","4069", "4xxx:4069").unit(1) #, Spice_Primitive="X", Spice_Model="4069UB", Spice_Node_Sequence="1 2 14 7")
  + (u1_dot_out := Junction())

  + Symbol("R3","100k", "Device:R").rotate(90) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y")
  + (u2_dot_in := Junction())
  + Symbol("U2","4069", "4xxx:4069").unit(1) #, Spice_Primitive="X", Spice_Model="4069UB", Spice_Node_Sequence="1 2 14 7")
  + (u2_dot_out := Junction())

  + Symbol("R5","10k", "Device:R").rotate(90) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y")
  + (u3_dot_in := Junction())
  + Symbol("U3","4069", "4xxx:4069").unit(1) #, Spice_Primitive="X", Spice_Model="4069UB", Spice_Node_Sequence="1 2 14 7")
  + (u3_dot_out := Junction())

  + Symbol ("R6","10k", "Device:R").rotate(90) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y")
  + (u4_dot_in := Junction())
  + Symbol("U4","4069", "4xxx:4069").unit(1) #, Spice_Primitive="X", Spice_Model="4069UB", Spice_Node_Sequence="1 2 14 7")
  + (u4_dot_out := Junction())

  + Wire().up().length(2*2.54).at(u1_dot_out)
  + Symbol("R2","100k", "Device:R").rotate(270).tox(u1_dot_in) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y"
  + (r2_out := Junction())
  + Wire().down().toy(u1_dot_in)

  + Wire().up().length(2*2.54).at(u2_dot_out)
  + LocalLabel("HP")
  + Symbol("R4","100k", "Device:R").rotate(270).tox(u2_dot_in) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y"
  + (r4_out := Junction())
  + Wire().down().toy(u2_dot_in)

  + Wire().up().length(2*2.54).at(u3_dot_out)
  + LocalLabel("BP")
  + (bp := Junction())
  + Symbol("C3","10n", "Device:C").rotate(270).tox(u3_dot_in) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y"
  + Wire().down().toy(u3_dot_in)

  + Wire().up().length(2*2.54).at(u4_dot_out)
  + LocalLabel("LP")
  + (lp := Junction())
  + Symbol("C4","10n", "Device:C").rotate(270).tox(u4_dot_in) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y"
  + Wire().down().toy(u4_dot_in)

  + Wire().up().length(2*2.54).at(lp)
  + Symbol("R7","100k", "Device:R").rotate(270).tox(r4_out) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y"
  + Wire().down().toy(r4_out)

  + Wire().up().length(4*2.54).at(bp)
  + Symbol("R8","100k", "Device:R").rotate(270).tox(r2_out) #, Spice_Netlist_Enabled="Y")
  + Wire().down().toy(r2_out)

  + Symbol("U1", "4069", "4xxx:4069").unit(7).at((50.8, 76.2)) #, Spice_Model="4069UB", Spice_Node_Sequence="1 2 14 7", on_schema="no"
  + Symbol("GND", "GND", "power:GND").unit(1).at("U1", "7") #, on_schema="no"
  + Symbol("+5V", "+5V", "power:+5V").unit(1).at("U1", "14") #, on_schema="no")

  + Symbol("U2", "4069", "4xxx:4069").unit(7).at((71.12, 76.2)) #, Spice_Model="4069UB", Spice_Node_Sequence="1 2 14 7", on_schema="no")
  + Symbol("GND", "GND", "power:GND").unit(1).at("U2", "7") #, on_schema="no"
  + Symbol("+5V", "+5V", "power:+5V").unit(1).at("U2", "14") #, on_schema="no"

  + Symbol("U3", "4069", "4xxx:4069").unit(7).at((91.44, 76.2)) #, Spice_Model="4069UB", Spice_Node_Sequence="1 2 14 7", on_schema="no")
  + Symbol("GND", "GND", "power:GND").unit(1).at("U3", "7") #, on_schema="no")
  + Symbol("+5V", "+5V", "power:+5V").unit(1).at("U3", "14") #p, on_schema="no"

  + Symbol("U4", "4069", "4xxx:4069").unit(7).at((111.76, 76.2)) # Spice_Model="4069UB", Spice_Node_Sequence="1 2 14 7", on_schema="no")
  + Symbol("GND", "GND", "power:GND").unit(1).at("U4", "7") # on_schema="no"
  + Symbol("+5V", "+5V", "power:+5V").unit(1).at("U4", "14") # on_schema="no"
)

draw.write("svf.kicad_sch")

#circuit = draw.circuit(["spice"])
#circuit.voltage("1", "+5V", "GND", "DC 15V")
#circuit.voltage("2", "INPUT", "GND", "AC 2V SIN(0 2V 1k)")
#circuit.control('''
#ac dec 10 100 10K
#
#*let r_act = 1k
#*let r_step = 2k
#*let r_stop = 200k
#*while r_act le r_stop
#*  alter R7 r_act
#*  alter R8 r_act
#*  ac dec 10 100 10K
#*  let r_act = r_act + r_step
#*end
#*tran 1us 10ms
#''')
#
#print("get simulation")
#simulation = Simulation(circuit)
#svf_data = simulation.tran("1us", "10ms", "0ms")
#print(len(svf_data))
#svf = simulation.run()
#
#for key, value in svf.items():
#  if key.startswith("ac"):
#    for k, v in value.items():
#      if k == "frequency":
#        svf[key][k] = v[1:]
#      else:
#        svf[key][k] = 20*np.log10(np.absolute(v))[1:]

draw.open()
draw.plot(scale=6)
```

This is the first setup with the 4069 as a voltage follower. C1 and C2 are DC blocking capacitors. When we choose R1 and R2 as 100kOhm we would expect a gain of one.

```{d3 element="svf_ac", x="frequency", y="bp,hp,lp" data="svf.ac1", width="600", height="400", fig.align="center", xType="scaleLog", fig.cap="Figure 7: State variable filter simulation"}```
